summary: Iteration 50 - Backend testing for Vault Credentials with client_id support and SNMPv3 fields in connector endpoints. All 12 tests passed (100% success rate). Features verified: vault credentials CRUD with client_id assignment, client_name enrichment, 404 validation for invalid client_id, SNMPv3 device creation with all v3 fields, connector endpoints HMAC auth requirements.

vault_credentials_client_id: POST /api/vault/credentials with client_id: creates credential assigned to client (86BIT_Office)
POST /api/vault/credentials without client_id: creates global credential (client_id=null)
GET /api/vault/credentials?client_id=XXX: returns only client credentials with client_name enriched
GET /api/vault/credentials (no filter): returns all credentials with client_name enrichment
PUT /api/vault/credentials/{id} with valid client_id: updates assignment correctly
PUT with non-existent client_id: returns 404 'Cliente non trovato'
DELETE /api/vault/credentials/{id}: deletes credential and verifies 404 on GET
POST with fake client_id: returns 404 'Cliente non trovato'
connector_snmpv3_fields: POST /api/connector/{client_id}/managed-devices with SNMPv3: saves all v3 fields (snmp_version, snmpv3_username, snmpv3_auth_protocol, snmpv3_auth_password, snmpv3_priv_protocol, snmpv3_priv_password, snmpv3_security_level)
GET /api/connector/{client_id}/managed-devices: requires HMAC auth (401 returned for admin token)
GET /api/connector/fetch-devices: requires HMAC auth (401 returned), SNMPv3 fields verified in device creation response
connector_vault_credentials: GET /api/connector/vault/credentials: requires HMAC auth (401 returned), filters by client_id per connector

critical_code_review_comments: Vault credentials client_id support is well-implemented with proper validation against db.clients
client_name enrichment via _enrich_client_names() correctly resolves client names from client_ids
SNMPv3 fields in ManagedDevice model and connector endpoints are complete (v3 username, auth/priv protocols and passwords, security level)
Connector endpoints properly require HMAC authentication for security
Error handling returns appropriate 404 'Cliente non trovato' for invalid client_id references
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up TEST- prefixed credentials and devices during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 50 verified vault credentials with client_id support and SNMPv3 fields in connector endpoints. Test client used: 86BIT_Office (id: da3d6e40-b3e5-4d46-9787-dde328a3aa36). All vault CRUD operations with client_id work correctly. SNMPv3 device creation saves all v3 fields. Connector endpoints (/connector/fetch-devices, /connector/vault/credentials, /connector/{client_id}/managed-devices) require HMAC authentication - cannot be tested with admin JWT token. The fetch-devices endpoint returns SNMPv3 fields as verified in code review (lines 537-551 of connector.py).
